# hmla

hmla is a browser-based generative ambient music synthesizer by Richard Solár.
It creates infinite, slowly-evolving ambient music from a short numeric seed (e.g. hmla-1234).
No samples or audio files — everything is synthesized live using Web Audio API via Tone.js.

The same seed always produces the same musical piece. Patches are shareable via URL.
Eight live faders (density, brightness, space, chaos, grain, sub, pulse, lofi) reshape sound in real time.
A shimmer toggle adds an octave-up reverb halo. Presets: calm, dense, puls, broken tape.
Output can be recorded and downloaded as WAV.

Live: https://hmla.richard.solar
